[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2019-06-17
[TITLE]: Toronto Raptors celebration parade marred by violence, transit shutdowns - The Globe and Mail
[METADATA/INTRO]: Enormous crowds filled downtown Toronto on Monday to cheer on their team as the city celebrated its first NBA Championship, and first major championship in more than 20 years – but the festivities were marred by several isolated incidents. Hundreds...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Enormous crowds of Raptors fans packed a route through Toronto’s downtown to see the NBA champions first-hand. Hours later than scheduled, the team arrived at Nathan Phillips Square where they thanked fans for the outpouring of support. 
 But authorities had estimated Sunday that close to two million people would show up to watch the parade. 
 Stephen Doig, a professor at Arizona State University and an expert on crowd-size estimation, looked at aerial photos of the crowds and estimated the number was likely 600,000 at the most. 
 “You are safe to say ‘hundreds of thousands’ came out to see the parade. That’s a fabulous crowd, and a great credit to the Raptors and their fans. But saying 'two million’ is, frankly, ridiculous," he said.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2015-05-22
[TITLE]: Watch: Liverpool FC Champions League 2005 homecoming parade - the incredible scenes as Reds brought trophy home - Liverpool Echo
[METADATA/INTRO]: They came, wave after wave, towards St George’s Hall. Those who believed it could happen, and those who didn’t. Those who had seen it all before, and those who were experiencing it for the first time. Those overjoyed, overwhelmed and...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The bus, naturally, was late. The turnout far exceeded expectations with around 750,000 estimated to have watched the parade – around 300,000 at St George’s Hall. But after waiting for 21 years, no matter. When the double-decker finally arrived, bedlam commenced, the city lit up by camera flashes in the dimming light. 
 After homecomings both red and blue of yesteryear, this was now the modern world – grainy images could now be captured on mobile phones and shared to the world. Our snappers were also on hand, with cameras of better quality, to capture the moments. 
 The parade would be over in a flash, the crowd dispersing far quicker than it had gathered. Hours of waiting for a finale lasting just minutes; a 21 year wait, over in three hours.
